summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is an octave?

Frequency and Musical Scales An octave = doubling of frequency. The other options — 'A halving of frequency', 'An increase of 100 Hz', and 'A change in amplitude' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

What is timbre?

Timbre (Tone Quality) Two instruments playing the same note sound different because of their harmonic content (overtones). The other options — 'The volume of a sound', 'The pitch of a note', and 'The speed of sound' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

A4 is 440 Hz. What is A5?

A4 = 440 Hz → A5 = 880 Hz. The other options — '440 Hz', '220 Hz', and '660 Hz' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

summary_sections.title: Special Topics
summary_sections.content_html:
Important Concepts

What frequency is concert A (A4)

A4 = 440 Hz → A5 = 880 Hz. The other options — '262 Hz', '880 Hz', and '1000 Hz' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

How does the ear perceive frequency

The human ear perceives frequency logarithmically — equal ratios sound like equal intervals. The other options — 'Linearly', 'Exponentially', and 'It doesn't perceive frequency' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
